title: Creamy Mexican Salt Cod Burrito
description: A fermented, pillowy, monsoon Mexican dish featuring salt cod, sashimi-cut, dash-friendly, in a burrito form, combining the flavors of Mexico with the richness of salt cod.
instructions: Desalt the salt cod by soaking it in cold water for 24 hours, changing the water several times.
Drain and flake the salt cod into small pieces.
In a large pan, heat the Mexican crema over medium heat until it starts to simmer.
Add the flaked salt cod to the crema and stir until it is fully coated.
Add the sashimi-cut onion, cilantro, and lime juice to the pan and stir to combine.
Warm the flour tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20 seconds.
Assemble the burritos by spooning the salt cod mixture onto the center of each tortilla, followed by a spoonful of diced tomatoes and a sprinkle of shredded cheese.
Fold the bottom edge of the tortilla up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the burrito up tightly.
Serve the burritos hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro.
